elements.title: Semois Valley National Park
elements.contents: ... that the Semois Valley National Park (pictured) and the National Park Entre-Sambre-et-Meuse together form part of one of the largest forested areas of Western Europe?

elements.title: Eorcengota
elements.contents: ... that Eorcengota had a vision predicting her own death in which she was symbolically represented as a gold coin?

elements.title: Bart Houston
elements.contents: ... that quarterback Bart Houston was named after quarterback Bart Starr?

elements.title: HMS Solebay
elements.contents: ... that HMS Solebay and HMS Derwent were the first ships assigned to suppress the Atlantic slave trade?

elements.title: Chilantaisaurus
elements.contents: ... that the Chinese dinosaur Chilantaisaurus is only known from a few giant bones including a huge hand claw?

elements.title: a statue at a baseball stadium
elements.contents: ... that a statue at a baseball stadium has been criticized for connections to racial segregation?

elements.title: the 2026 Boat Race
elements.contents: ... that Cambridge won all but one of the eight events at the 2026 Boat Race, the exception being the Oxford women's team, who broke their streak of eight consecutive losses?

elements.title: Vol. II
elements.contents: ... that a critic scored Angine de Poitrine's album Vol. II a perfect 10 despite calling it "pretentious and performative"?

elements.title: Helmac Products Corporation
elements.contents: ... that Nicholas McKay formed the Helmac Products Corporation in 1956 to manufacture and sell his invention: the lint roller?
